meta.title: Security Architecture Deep Dive
meta.excerpt: Implementing defense in depth with four security layers: origin validation, rate limiting, input sanitization, and schema validation

props.body: Security is not a feature - it is a requirement. Our approach implements defense in depth: multiple layers that each catch different threats. If one layer fails, others still protect the system.

props.text: No single security measure is foolproof. We implement four distinct layers, each targeting different attack vectors. An attacker must bypass ALL layers to succeed.

Every Server Action first validates that the request comes from our application. This prevents Cross-Site Request Forgery (CSRF) attacks where malicious sites trick users into submitting forms to our server.

props.title: Why Both Headers?
props.body: Some browsers do not send the Origin header for same-origin requests. We check both Origin and Referer headers to ensure legitimate requests are not blocked while still preventing CSRF.

props.text: Rate limiting prevents abuse by restricting how many requests a client can make. Each form type has different limits based on expected legitimate usage patterns.
All string inputs are sanitized before processing. This prevents Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) attacks where malicious scripts are injected through form fields.

props.title: Sanitize on Server, Not Just Client
props.body: Client-side sanitization can be bypassed. Always sanitize on the server before storing or using data. Client-side sanitization is only for immediate display feedback.

props.text: After sanitization, Zod schemas validate data structure, types, and business rules. This catches malformed data that passed previous layers.

features.title: Type Validation
features.description: Ensures strings, numbers, booleans, and enums match expected types.

features.title: Constraint Checking
features.description: Length limits, format patterns (email, phone), and range boundaries.

features.title: Business Rules
features.description: Custom validation like 'emergency requires phone' or 'date must be future'.

features.title: Structured Errors
features.description: Field-level error messages that map directly to form UI.

props.text: Every Server Action runs through this complete pipeline. The securityCheck function wraps all checks into a single reusable utility.

steps.title: Schema Validation
steps.description: Verify data structure and business rules

idealFor: CSRF (Cross-Site Request Forgery) via origin validation
notIdealFor: SQL Injection - add parameterized queries with database
Session hijacking - implement secure session management
Password attacks - add bcrypt hashing for auth

props.body: Each security layer catches what others miss. Origin validation stops CSRF, rate limiting prevents abuse, sanitization neutralizes XSS, and schema validation enforces data integrity. Defense in depth is not paranoia - it is engineering discipline. When you add authentication and database access, you will add more layers to this foundation.
